title: Knot contact homology and partially wrapped Floer homology
speaker: Lenhard Ng
tags: Homological Mirror Symmetry: Methods and Structures

proof_stages.label: Legendrian conormal torus of a knot
proof_stages.summary: The talk constructs from a smooth knot K in R^3 its Lagrangian conormal L_K in the disk cotangent bundle and its Legendrian boundary torus lambda_K in the unit cotangent bundle, noting that smooth isotopy of knots gives Legendrian isotopy of the tori even though all such tori are smoothly isotopic as ordinary submanifolds.

proof_stages.label: Completeness theorem and sheaf-theoretic recognition route
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem is stated: if lambda_K and lambda_K' are Legendrian isotopic then K and K' are smoothly isotopic, with Shende's earlier proof sketched via constructible sheaves extracting the knot-group ring, left-orderability recovering the group, and peripheral data plus Waldhausen recovering the knot.

proof_stages.label: Chekanov-Eliashberg DGA and knot contact homology
proof_stages.summary: Legendrian contact homology is reviewed by defining the Chekanov-Eliashberg DGA from Reeb chords and holomorphic disks, proving its Legendrian-isotopy invariance, and applying it to lambda_K to obtain knot contact homology, whose completeness is still unknown.

proof_stages.label: Enhancement by adding a cotangent fiber
proof_stages.summary: A point p in the knot complement is chosen and its cotangent-fiber Legendrian sphere lambda_p is added to lambda_K, producing an enhanced Legendrian link whose contact homology remains a smooth knot invariant.

proof_stages.label: Mixed-chord algebra A_{pKp} and product
proof_stages.summary: Inside the DGA of lambda_K union lambda_p the speaker isolates the subquotient A_{pKp} generated by words with an initial p-to-K mixed chord, internal K-to-K chords, and a final K-to-p mixed chord, explains why the differential descends, and equips it with a ring structure by counting disks with two positive ends.

proof_stages.label: Group-ring computation of the enhanced algebra
proof_stages.summary: A key theorem identifies the lowest-degree homology of A_{pKp}, after adjoining a unit, with the group ring Z[pi_K] of the knot group, using a comparison with string topology.

proof_stages.label: Recovery of the knot from the enhanced invariant
proof_stages.summary: Because the enhanced contact-homology package determines the group ring and, with extra work, the meridian and longitude, the earlier group-theoretic and Waldhausen recognition argument yields the complete knot invariant result.

proof_stages.label: Partially wrapped Floer reinterpretation
proof_stages.summary: The enhanced mixed-chord algebra is reinterpreted as the self-Floer complex of the cotangent disk fiber L_p in a cotangent bundle whose wrapping is stopped by lambda_K, so that the group-ring theorem becomes a natural partially wrapped Floer homology computation.

examples.name: Links as a questioned extension
examples.note: An audience question about links serves as an optional aside clarifying that group-ring or knot-group data must be supplemented by peripheral longitude/meridian information.
examples.engagement_reason: links arise only in an audience question and the speaker speculates about whether the knot result extends, without working through any link example or proof

passes.reason: links are raised only as a possible/unknown extension of the knot result, not as an exhibited failure case
Links are raised only as a possible extension of the knot theorem, not used to disprove a claim, and are structurally ordinary members of the link class.
links are raised only as a possible extension/general setting, not exhibited as a failing case
Links are raised as an ordinary possible generalization of the knot result, with no specific failure exhibited.
links are the standard multi-component generalization of knots, raised only as an uncertain extension of the theorem rather than as a failure example
Links are only raised as an ordinary possible extension of the knot result, with no specific link or failure exhibited.
Links are raised only as a possible extension of the knot result, not as an asserted failure case, and ordinary links have no special extremal, degenerate, symmetric, or sporadic structure here.
Links are raised only as a possible extension or uncertain failure case, not as an exhibited counterexample, and are structurally ordinary among link objects.
Links are raised only as an uncertain possible extension of the knot result, not as a known failing case.
Links are raised only as a possible/unknown extension of the knot result, not as an exhibited failure, and a general link is not structurally sporadic, extremal, degenerate, or maximally symmetric.
Links are an ordinary generalization of knots, raised only as an uncertain extension rather than an exhibited failure.
Links are raised only as an unresolved possible extension of the knot result, and a general link is structurally ordinary.

examples.name: Cotangent-fiber wrapped Floer homology and loop spaces
examples.note: The standard wrapped-Floer computation for a cotangent fiber in a cotangent bundle is cited as the inspiration for interpreting the enhanced invariant as a stopped/partially wrapped Floer algebra computing a group ring.
examples.engagement_reason: the cotangent-fiber wrapped Floer homology/loop-space identification is cited as inspirational background, not derived or computed in that case

passes.reason: the cotangent-fiber wrapped Floer/loop-space calculation is a standard motivating model, not a sporadic, limiting, optimal, maximally symmetric, or refuting example
A cotangent fiber and its wrapped Floer homology are used as a standard motivating model for the loop-space interpretation, not as a failure case or structurally exceptional object.
cotangent-fiber wrapped Floer homology is used as a standard motivating model relating to loop spaces, not as a counterexample
A cotangent fiber in a cotangent bundle is a standard Lagrangian object used as a positive model relating wrapped Floer homology to loop spaces.
the cotangent fiber wrapped-Floer/loop-space identification is a standard model computation used for inspiration, not a structurally sporadic or adversarial case
The cotangent fiber wrapped Floer homology/loop-space identification is a standard motivating case, not a structural exception or a disproof.

The wrapped Floer homology of a cotangent fiber is used as a standard motivating model relating Floer theory to loop spaces, not as a counterexample, and the cotangent fiber is a typical standard Lagrangian rather than extremal, degenerate, maximally symmetric, or exceptional.
The cotangent-fiber wrapped Floer/loop-space correspondence is used as a motivating standard model, not to refute a claim, and the fiber is a regular member of an infinite standard family.
A cotangent fiber in wrapped Floer homology is used as the standard motivating model relating Floer theory to loop spaces.

The cotangent-fiber wrapped Floer/loop-space comparison is used as a motivating standard model, and a cotangent fiber is a canonical but not sporadic, extremal, degenerate, or maximally symmetric Lagrangian object here.
A cotangent fiber is the standard exact Lagrangian model used as inspiration for wrapped Floer homology computations, not a counterexample or sporadic/extremal object.
The cotangent-fiber wrapped Floer/loop-space correspondence is used as motivating standard model, not as a failure case, and the fiber is a regular non-sporadic Lagrangian example.
